name: Jinja2
what_it_does: Python templating engine — renders text templates with variables, control structures, and filters. Jinja2 features: Environment for template loading, FileSystemLoader/PackageLoader for template files, template inheritance ({% extends %}, {% block %}), macros ({% macro %}), include ({% include %}), filters ({{ name|upper }}, {{ list|join(', ') }}), tests ({% if x is defined %}), whitespace control (-), Undefined behavior config, autoescape for HTML security, async template rendering, and custom extensions. Used in Flask, Ansible, Airflow, and agent prompt template systems.
use_cases: Agent prompt templates — env = Environment(loader=FileSystemLoader('prompts/')); template = env.get_template('research.j2'); prompt = template.render(topic=task.topic, context=retrieved_docs, max_length=2000) — parameterized agent prompts from version-controlled template files

Agent report generation — template.render(agents=active_agents, date=today, metrics=dashboard_data) renders HTML agent dashboard report; Jinja2 loop {% for agent in agents %} with conditionals {% if agent.status == 'error' %}; agent status reports as HTML email

Code generation for agent tools — Jinja2 templates generate Python/YAML/SQL boilerplate; agent scaffolding CLI renders agent_class.py.j2 with agent name and capabilities; Ansible uses Jinja2 for dynamic agent infrastructure templates

Flask agent web UI — render_template('agent/detail.html', agent=agent, tasks=tasks) passes agent data to Jinja2; template inheritance via {% extends 'base.html' %} shares agent layout; Flask's render_template wraps Jinja2 Environment

Agent email templating — body = email_template.render(agent_name=agent.name, error=error_msg, link=dashboard_url) renders agent alert emails; separates email content from Python logic; template files editable without code deploy
not_for: React/Vue frontends — Jinja2 is server-side Python; for component-based frontends use React/Vue with JSX/SFC templates
Real-time dynamic updates — Jinja2 renders once server-side; for live-updating agent dashboards use WebSockets + JavaScript or Hotwire
High-security user-generated templates — Jinja2 Sandbox restricts but doesn't eliminate code execution risk; never render user-provided template strings in production agent systems without sandboxing
best_when: Your Python agent service needs server-side HTML rendering, parameterized prompt templates stored as files, code generation, or configuration templating — Jinja2 is Python's most widely used template engine with excellent documentation and ecosystem support.
avoid_when: You need frontend components, real-time rendering, or are rendering untrusted user-provided template strings without sandboxing.

auth.notes: No auth — local Python library for template rendering.

pricing.notes: Jinja2 is BSD-3-Clause licensed, maintained by the Pallets project. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — in-process template rendering. Template compilation cached by Jinja2 Environment. Bytecode cache (FileSystemBytecodeCache) stores compiled templates across restarts for faster server startup.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: TemplateNotFound shows template name and search path. UndefinedError shows variable name that's undefined in template. TemplateSyntaxError shows line number and syntax issue. TypeError in template filter shows filter name and input type. All errors include template name and line number.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Template rendering is pure — same template + same context = same output. Fully idempotent. Environment caches compiled templates — repeated render calls use cached bytecode.
known_agent_gotchas: autoescape disabled by default — Environment() without autoescape=True doesn't escape HTML; agent templates rendering user content without autoescape are XSS-vulnerable; always use autoescape=True for HTML templates; Flask's render_template enables autoescape for .html files automatically

Undefined variables silently render as empty string — Jinja2 Undefined renders as '' by default; agent templates with typo in variable name silently produce wrong output; use Environment(undefined=StrictUndefined) to raise UndefinedError on missing variables; catches agent template variable name mismatches

Template inheritance requires careful block structure — {% block content %}...{% endblock %} in child template replaces parent block; forgetting {{ super() }} loses parent block content; agent email templates using base layout must call super() to include parent HTML structure

Jinja2 in Ansible vs Python API differ — Ansible Jinja2 syntax {{ var }} works in YAML files; Python Jinja2 Environment renders Python strings; agent automation scripts mixing Ansible playbooks and Python Jinja2 code have subtle filter and test differences (Ansible adds custom filters not in Python Jinja2)

Loop variables require loop. prefix — {% for item in items %}{{ loop.index }} {{ item }}{% endfor %} — loop.index (1-based), loop.index0 (0-based), loop.first, loop.last; agent templates using index without loop. prefix get variable not found; Jinja2 loop variable differs from Python enumerate()

Whitespace control matters for non-HTML — {%- if condition -%} strips whitespace around block; agent code generation templates without whitespace control produce unexpected blank lines; YAML/JSON templates generated by Jinja2 fail YAML parsing if extra newlines are inserted; use - in tags for code generation templates

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: autoescape=True required for HTML templates to prevent XSS. NEVER render user-provided template strings with Environment().from_string() in agent apps — enables server-side template injection (SSTI), arbitrary code execution. Use Environment(sandbox=True) (SandboxedEnvironment) for user-provided templates, though sandbox is not a complete security boundary.
